macro_events.event_summary: NATO Secretary General Rutte announced hundreds of billions in new alliance defense spending while Trump pledged 5,000 additional US troops to Poland, the alliance's top defense spender.
primary_drivers: Increased NATO/European defense procurement budgets flowing to US prime contractors
Higher US troop forward deployment driving logistics, munitions, and equipment demand
Sustained multi-year backlog growth for missile defense, air defense, and ammunition makers

beneficiaries.rationale: Lockheed Martin is the prime on F-35 (Poland operates F-35s and is expanding), PAC-3 missiles, and HIMARS — all top procurement items for Poland and frontline NATO states. Direct backlog tailwind from European spending step-up.

Raytheon supplies Patriot air defense systems (Poland's Wisla program) and AMRAAM/Stinger missiles; NATO spending hike directly expands Patriot interceptor and radar orders. Also supplies engines via Pratt.
General Dynamics makes Abrams tanks (Poland ordered 250+ M1A2s) and ammunition through OTS; troop deployment to Poland increases armored vehicle support and 155mm shell demand.

Northrop Grumman supplies IBCS (Poland's integrated air/missile defense command system), Triton/Global Hawk surveillance, and is a key munitions/solid rocket motor supplier — direct Polish program exposure.
Leidos provides logistics, IT and base support services to US forces abroad; 5,000 additional troops in Poland expands its forward-deployment services contract base.
Huntington Ingalls benefits indirectly via broader US defense topline expansion as NATO commitments pressure overall US budget upward, though naval exposure is less Poland-specific.
Kratos supplies target drones, tactical drones, and hypersonic test systems used in expanding NATO training and missile defense testing; smaller-cap leverage to the spending wave.
AeroVironment's Switchblade loitering munitions and small UAS have been heavily used/ordered by NATO eastern flank members post-Ukraine; NATO budget hike sustains the order pipeline.

headlines: NATO will spend hundreds of billions of dollars on defense, says Rutte, as Trump pledges 5,000 American troops to top spender Poland
